assertion: deepseek-r1:14b is 2-7x faster than qwen2.5-coder:32b across all task categories on M4 Max, while achieving comparable completion rates (18/19 vs 19/19)
evidence: Reasoning: DS avg 54s vs QW avg 172s (3.2x faster)
Summarization: DS avg 18s vs QW avg 46s (2.6x faster)
Structured output: DS avg 29s vs QW avg 88s (3.0x faster)
Code gen: DS avg 163s vs QW avg 223s (1.4x faster, but DS missed CG-4)
R-5 Adaptive Routing: DS 27s vs QW 193s (7.1x faster — largest gap)
CG-4 PoT Verifier: DS timeout 300s vs QW 165s (only QW win)

assertion: qwen2.5-coder:32b achieves perfect 19/19 completion on Kingdom-representative tasks but at 2-7x the latency of deepseek-r1:14b, making it better suited for quality-critical batch work than real-time use
evidence: Total benchmark time: 43 min vs 22 min for deepseek
CG-4 completed at 165s where deepseek timed out at 300s
Consistent quality — no visible thinking traces in output (cleaner parsing)

assertion: deepseek-r1:14b is the optimal default for Kingdom adaptive routing on M4 Max — fastest across all categories, with qwen2.5-coder:32b as fallback for tasks requiring higher reliability or where deepseek times out
evidence: DS reasoning tasks avg 54s (vs QW 172s) — best category advantage
QW 19/19 reliability advantage — use as fallback for complex code gen
Recommended routing: DS first (cheap+fast), QW fallback on timeout/failure

assertion: deepseek-r1:14b's visible chain-of-thought output makes it unsuitable for API-style use without output filtering — the 'Thinking...' preamble adds noise and latency, especially on complex code generation tasks
evidence: All 19 tasks included 'Thinking...' preamble before actual output
CG-1 Cosmos SDK: ~50% of 271s elapsed was chain-of-thought
CG-4 timeout directly caused by thinking overhead extending past 300s deadline
qwen2.5-coder:32b produces clean output without thinking traces — easier integration
